data: Marble plaque damaged on the left and on the right, set in plaster in modern times.
A with broken bar, Δ with serif, lunate Ε and Σ, cursive Ω.
Original discovery not recorded, according to Korhonen and Noy from Catania.
Observed by Libertini in Museo Biscari, later by Korhonen in Magazzino del cortile, Collezione Biscari (formerly sala VII 288)
I.Sicily with the permission of the Assessorato Regionale dei Beni Culturali e dell’Identità Siciliana - Dipartimento dei Beni Culturali e dell’Identità Siciliana
Castello Ursino, Piazza Federico II di Svevia, 95121 Catania (CT)
Le iscrizioni del Museo civico di Catania : storia delle collezioni, cultura epigrafica, edizione
Kalle Korhonen, Le iscrizioni del Museo civico di Catania : storia delle collezioni, cultura epigrafica, edizione (Helsinki: Societas Scientiarum Fennica, 2004)
Guido Libertini, Il Museo Biscari (Milan: Casa editrice D’arte Bestetti e Tuminelli, 1930)
Antonio Ferrua, «Osservazioni sulle iscrizioni cristiane catanesi», Bollettino Storico Catanese 3 (1938): 60–74
D. Noy, Jewish Inscriptions of Western Europe, 2 vols (Cambridge: Cambridge University Press, 1993)
Kalle Korhonen, «Osservazioni sul collezionismo epigrafico siciliano», Arctos: acta philologica fennica 35 (2001): 85–102
Robert Crellin integrated lemmatizations into EpiDoc using PyEpiDoc and associated scripts
Ferrua, 1938, Osservazioni sulle iscrizioni cristiane catanesi
Noy, 1993, Jewish inscriptions of Western Europe
Korhonen, 2001, Osservazioni sul collezionismo epigrafico siciliano

1[Πρ]ῖσκα
2[ἔζ]ησεν
3[ἔτ]η δέκ-
4[αοκ?]τῶ´ 🕎

Text encoding: Dmitry Dundua, Robert Crellin
Πρῖσκα ἔζησεν ἔτη δέκαοκτῶ

Text encoding: Marta Fogagnolo, Alex Antoniou
1[..]ΙΣΚΑ
2[..]ΗΣΕΝ
3[..]ΗΔΕΚ
4[...]ΤΩ🕎

Text encoding: Dmitry Dundua, Robert Crellin
ΠΡΙΣΚΑΕΖΗΣΕΝΕΤΗΔΕΚΑΟΚΤΩ

Facsimile Images

Apparatus criticus

  • Text based on photograph ;
  • line.1: Ferrua, Noy: [---]ις κα[---]; Korhonen: [Πρ]ῖσκα
  • line.2: Libertini, Korhonen: [ἔζ]ησεν; Ferrua, Noy: [---]ης εν[---]
  • line.3: Liberini, Noy: δέκ[α]; Ferrua [---]ηδε κ[---]; Korhonen: [ἔτ]η δέκ[α]
  • line.4: Ferrua: ἐτω(ν); Korhonen: [ἐν Χρισ]τῷ (the space between τω and the previous letter could be justified if we suppose an abbreviation: ἐν Χρ(ισ)τῷ); Chaniotis: [μήνας ὀκ]τώ ;
  • Ferrua, Noy and Korhonen 2001 interpreted the symbol as a menorah, but later Korhonen as a palm

English translation

Marta Fogagnolo

Prisca lived for 18 (?) years.

Italian translation

Marta Fogagnolo

Prisca visse per 18 (?) anni.

commentary

The epitaph is Jewish, as revealed by the symbol of a menorah. Korhonen rejects the Judaic provenance of the inscription believing the symbol to be a palm, but the feet of the menorah seems to be present even if not rubricated. Korhonen’s supplement to l. 4 ἐν Χρ(ισ)τῷ (the same abbreviation as in RQUA 10 (1896) 38 nr. 67), for this reason, cannot be supported: moreover, the space does not seem sufficient. If one assumes that a few characters are missing on the right, the integration of Chaniotis (ap. SEG) [μήνας ὀκ]τώ is also not possible. One possibility would be to integrate the numeral δεκαοκτώ. For the name Prisca in another Latin inscription from Catania from the imperial period, see CIL 10.1088*199 = ISic003241.

  • Korhonen 2001 93-94 https://biblio.inscriptiones.org/epig10001425
  • Korhonen 2004 192 https://biblio.inscriptiones.org/epig10000028
